verses.text: When he had set the brands on fire, he let them go into the standing grain of the Philistines, and burnt up both the shocks and the standing grain, and also the olive groves.

verses.name: Judges 15:6
verses.text: Then the Philistines said, “Who has done this?”   They said, “Samson, the son-in-law of the Timnite, because he has taken his wife, and given her to his companion.” The Philistines came up, and burnt her and her father with fire.

verses.name: Judges 15:7
verses.text: Samson said to them, “If you behave like this, surely I will be avenged of you, and after that I will cease.”

verses.name: Judges 15:8
verses.text: He struck them hip and thigh with a great slaughter: and he went down and lived in the cleft of the rock of Etam.

verses.name: Judges 15:9
verses.text: Then the Philistines went up, and encamped in Judah, and spread themselves in Lehi.

verses.name: Judges 15:10
verses.text: The men of Judah said, “Why have you come up against us?”   They said, “We have come up to bind Samson, to do to him as he has done to us.”

verses.name: Judges 15:11
verses.text: Then three thousand men of Judah went down to the cleft of the rock of Etam, and said to Samson, “Don’t you know that the Philistines are rulers over us? What then is this that you have done to us?”   He said to them, “As they did to me, so have I done to them.”

verses.name: Judges 15:12
verses.text: They said to him, “We have come down to bind you, that we may deliver you into the hand of the Philistines.”   Samson said to them, “Swear to me that you will not fall on me yourselves.”

verses.name: Judges 15:13
verses.text: They spoke to him, saying, “No; but we will bind you fast, and deliver you into their hand; but surely we will not kill you.” They bound him with two new ropes, and brought him up from the rock.

verses.name: Judges 15:14
verses.text: When he came to Lehi, the Philistines shouted as they met him: and the Spirit of Yahweh came mightily on him, and the ropes that were on his arms became as flax that was burnt with fire, and his bands dropped from off his hands.

verses.name: Judges 15:15
verses.text: He found a fresh jawbone of a donkey, and put out his hand, and took it, and struck a thousand men with it.

verses.name: Judges 15:16
verses.text: Samson said, “With the jawbone of a donkey, heaps on heaps; with the jawbone of a donkey I have struck a thousand men.”

verses.name: Judges 15:17
verses.text: When he had finished speaking, he threw the jawbone out of his hand; and that place was called Ramath Lehi.

verses.name: Judges 15:18
verses.text: He was very thirsty, and called on Yahweh, and said, “You have given this great deliverance by the hand of your servant; and now shall I die for thirst, and fall into the hand of the uncircumcised?”

verses.name: Judges 15:19
verses.text: But God split the hollow place that is in Lehi, and water came out of it. When he had drunk, his spirit came again, and he revived: therefore its name was called En Hakkore, which is in Lehi, to this day.

verses.name: Judges 15:20
verses.text: He judged Israel in the days of the Philistines twenty years.

verses.name: Judges 16:1
verses.text: Samson went to Gaza, and saw there a prostitute, and went in to her.

verses.name: Judges 16:2
verses.text: The Gazites were told, “Samson is here!” They surrounded him, and laid wait for him all night in the gate of the city, and were quiet all the night, saying, “Wait until morning light, then we will kill him.”

verses.name: Judges 16:3
verses.text: Samson lay until midnight, and arose at midnight, and laid hold of the doors of the gate of the city, and the two posts, and plucked them up, bar and all, and put them on his shoulders, and carried them up to the top of the mountain that is before Hebron.

verses.name: Judges 16:4
verses.text: It came to pass afterward, that he loved a woman in the valley of Sorek, whose name was Delilah.

verses.name: Judges 16:5
verses.text: The lords of the Philistines came up to her, and said to her, “Entice him, and see in which his great strength lies, and by what means we may prevail against him, that we may bind him to afflict him; and we will each give you eleven hundred pieces of silver.”

verses.name: Judges 16:6
verses.text: Delilah said to Samson, “Please tell me where your great strength lies, and what you might be bound to afflict you.”

verses.name: Judges 16:7
verses.text: Samson said to her, “If they bind me with seven green cords that were never dried, then shall I become weak, and be as another man.”

verses.name: Judges 16:8
verses.text: Then the lords of the Philistines brought up to her seven green cords which had not been dried, and she bound him with them.

verses.name: Judges 16:9
verses.text: Now she had an ambush waiting in the inner room. She said to him, “The Philistines are on you, Samson!” He broke the cords, as a string of tow is broken when it touches the fire. So his strength was not known.

verses.name: Judges 16:10
verses.text: Delilah said to Samson, “Behold, you have mocked me, and told me lies: now please tell me with which you might be bound.”

verses.name: Judges 16:11
verses.text: He said to her, “If they only bind me with new ropes with which no work has been done, then shall I become weak, and be as another man.”

verses.name: Judges 16:12
verses.text: So Delilah took new ropes, and bound him therewith, and said to him, “The Philistines are on you, Samson!” The ambush was waiting in the inner room. He broke them off his arms like a thread.

verses.name: Judges 16:13
verses.text: Delilah said to Samson, “Until now, you have mocked me and told me lies. Tell me with what you might be bound.”   He said to her, “If you weave the seven locks of my head with the web.”

verses.name: Judges 16:14
verses.text: She fastened it with the pin, and said to him, “The Philistines are on you, Samson!” He awakened out of his sleep, and plucked away the pin of the beam, and the web.

verses.name: Judges 16:15
verses.text: She said to him, “How can you say, ‘I love you,’ when your heart is not with me? You have mocked me these three times, and have not told me where your great strength lies.”

verses.name: Judges 16:16
verses.text: When she pressed him daily with her words, and urged him, his soul was troubled to death.

verses.name: Judges 16:17
verses.text: He told her all his heart, and said to her, “No razor has ever come on my head; for I have been a Nazirite to God from my mother’s womb. If I am shaved, then my strength will go from me, and I will become weak, and be like any other man.”

verses.name: Judges 16:18
verses.text: When Delilah saw that he had told her all his heart, she sent and called for the lords of the Philistines, saying, “Come up this once, for he has told me all his heart.” Then the lords of the Philistines came up to her, and brought the money in their hand.

verses.name: Judges 16:19
verses.text: She made him sleep on her knees; and she called for a man, and shaved off the seven locks of his head; and she began to afflict him, and his strength went from him.

verses.name: Judges 16:20
verses.text: She said, “The Philistines are upon you, Samson!”   He awoke out of his sleep, and said, “I will go out as at other times, and shake myself free.” But he didn’t know that Yahweh had departed from him.

verses.name: Judges 16:21
verses.text: The Philistines laid hold on him, and put out his eyes; and they brought him down to Gaza, and bound him with fetters of brass; and he ground at the mill in the prison.

verses.name: Judges 16:22
verses.text: However the hair of his head began to grow again after he was shaved.

verses.name: Judges 16:23
verses.text: The lords of the Philistines gathered them together to offer a great sacrifice to Dagon their god, and to rejoice; for they said, “Our god has delivered Samson our enemy into our hand.”

verses.name: Judges 16:24
verses.text: When the people saw him, they praised their god; for they said, “Our god has delivered our enemy and the destroyer of our country, who has slain many of us, into our hand.”

verses.name: Judges 16:25
verses.text: When their hearts were merry, they said, “Call for Samson, that he may entertain us.” They called for Samson out of the prison; and he performed before them. They set him between the pillars;

verses.name: Judges 16:26
verses.text: and Samson said to the boy who held him by the hand, “Allow me to feel the pillars whereupon the house rests, that I may lean on them.”

verses.name: Judges 16:27
verses.text: Now the house was full of men and women; and all the lords of the Philistines were there; and there were on the roof about three thousand men and women, who saw while Samson performed.

verses.name: Judges 16:28
verses.text: Samson called to Yahweh, and said, “Lord Yahweh, remember me, please, and strengthen me, please, only this once, God, that I may be at once avenged of the Philistines for my two eyes.”

verses.name: Judges 16:29
verses.text: Samson took hold of the two middle pillars on which the house rested, and leaned on them, the one with his right hand, and the other with his left.

verses.name: Judges 16:30
verses.text: Samson said, “Let me die with the Philistines!” He bowed himself with all his might; and the house fell on the lords, and on all the people who were therein. So the dead that he killed at his death were more than those who he killed in his life.

verses.name: Judges 16:31
verses.text: Then his brothers and all the house of his father came down, and took him, and brought him up, and buried him between Zorah and Eshtaol in the burial site of Manoah his father. He judged Israel twenty years.

verses.name: Judges 17:1
verses.text: There was a man of the hill country of Ephraim, whose name was Micah.

verses.name: Judges 17:2
verses.text: He said to his mother, “The eleven hundred pieces of silver that were taken from you, about which you uttered a curse, and also spoke it in my ears, behold, the silver is with me; I took it.”   His mother said, “Blessed be my son of Yahweh.”

verses.name: Judges 17:3
verses.text: He restored the eleven hundred pieces of silver to his mother; and his mother said, “I most certainly dedicate the silver to Yahweh from my hand for my son, to make an engraved image and a molten image. Now therefore I will restore it to you.”

verses.name: Judges 17:4
verses.text: When he restored the money to his mother, his mother took two hundred pieces of silver, and gave them to the founder, who made of it an engraved image and a molten image: and it was in the house of Micah.

verses.name: Judges 17:5
verses.text: The man Micah had a house of gods, and he made an ephod, and teraphim, and consecrated one of his sons, who became his priest.

verses.name: Judges 17:6
verses.text: In those days there was no king in Israel: every man did that which was right in his own eyes.

verses.name: Judges 17:7
verses.text: There was a young man out of Bethlehem Judah, of the family of Judah, who was a Levite; and he lived there.

verses.name: Judges 17:8
verses.text: The man departed out of the city, out of Bethlehem Judah, to live where he could find a place, and he came to the hill country of Ephraim to the house of Micah, as he traveled.

verses.name: Judges 17:9
verses.text: Micah said to him, “Where did you come from?”   He said to him, “I am a Levite of Bethlehem Judah, and I am looking for a place to live.”

verses.name: Judges 17:10
verses.text: Micah said to him, “Dwell with me, and be to me a father and a priest, and I will give you ten pieces of silver per year, a suit of clothing, and your food.” So the Levite went in.

verses.name: Judges 17:11
verses.text: The Levite was content to dwell with the man; and the young man was to him as one of his sons.

verses.name: Judges 17:12
verses.text: Micah consecrated the Levite, and the young man became his priest, and was in the house of Micah.

verses.name: Judges 17:13
verses.text: Then Micah said, “Now know I that Yahweh will do good to me, since I have a Levite to my priest.”

verses.name: Judges 18:1
verses.text: In those days there was no king in Israel: and in those days the tribe of the Danites sought an inheritance to dwell in; for to that day, their inheritance had not fallen to them among the tribes of Israel.

verses.name: Judges 18:2
verses.text: The children of Dan sent of their family five men from their whole number, men of valor, from Zorah, and from Eshtaol, to spy out the land, and to search it; and they said to them, “Go, explore the land!”   They came to the hill country of Ephraim, to the house of Micah, and lodged there.

verses.name: Judges 18:3
verses.text: When they were by the house of Micah, they knew the voice of the young man the Levite; and they turned aside there, and said to him, “Who brought you here? What do you do in this place? What do you have here?”

verses.name: Judges 18:4
verses.text: He said to them, “Thus and thus has Micah dealt with me, and he has hired me, and I am become his priest.”

verses.name: Judges 18:5
verses.text: They said to him, “Please ask counsel of God, that we may know whether our way which we go shall be prosperous.”

verses.name: Judges 18:6
verses.text: The priest said to them, “Go in peace. Your way in which you go is before Yahweh.”

verses.name: Judges 18:7
verses.text: Then the five men departed, and came to Laish, and saw the people who were therein, how they lived in security, in the way of the Sidonians, quiet and secure; for there was no one in the land, possessing authority, that might put them to shame in anything, and they were far from the Sidonians, and had no dealings with any man.

verses.name: Judges 18:8
verses.text: They came to their brothers to Zorah and Eshtaol: and their brothers said to them, “What do you say?”

verses.name: Judges 18:9
verses.text: They said, “Arise, and let us go up against them; for we have seen the land, and behold, it is very good. Do you stand still? Don’t be slothful to go and to enter in to possess the land.

verses.name: Judges 18:10
verses.text: When you go, you shall come to a secure people, and the land is large; for God has given it into your hand, a place where there is no want of anything that is in the earth.”

verses.name: Judges 18:11
verses.text: There set out from there of the family of the Danites, out of Zorah and out of Eshtaol, six hundred men girt with weapons of war.

verses.name: Judges 18:12
verses.text: They went up, and encamped in Kiriath Jearim, in Judah: therefore they called that place Mahaneh Dan, to this day; behold, it is behind Kiriath Jearim.

verses.name: Judges 18:13
verses.text: They passed there to the hill country of Ephraim, and came to the house of Micah.

verses.name: Judges 18:14
verses.text: Then the five men who went to spy out the country of Laish answered, and said to their brothers, “Do you know that there is in these houses an ephod, and teraphim, and an engraved image, and a molten image? Now therefore consider what you have to do.”

verses.name: Judges 18:15
verses.text: They turned aside there, and came to the house of the young man the Levite, even to the house of Micah, and asked him of his welfare.

verses.name: Judges 18:16
verses.text: The six hundred men girt with their weapons of war, who were of the children of Dan, stood by the entrance of the gate.

verses.name: Judges 18:17
verses.text: The five men who went to spy out the land went up, and came in there, and took the engraved image, and the ephod, and the teraphim, and the molten image: and the priest stood by the entrance of the gate with the six hundred men girt with weapons of war.

verses.name: Judges 18:18
verses.text: When these went into Micah’s house, and fetched the engraved image, the ephod, and the teraphim, and the molten image, the priest said to them, “What are you doing?”

verses.name: Judges 18:19
verses.text: They said to him, “Hold your peace, put your hand on your mouth, and go with us, and be to us a father and a priest. Is it better for you to be priest to the house of one man, or to be priest to a tribe and a family in Israel?”

verses.name: Judges 18:20
verses.text: The priest’s heart was glad, and he took the ephod, and the teraphim, and the engraved image, and went in the midst of the people.

verses.name: Judges 18:21
verses.text: So they turned and departed, and put the little ones and the livestock and the goods before them.

verses.name: Judges 18:22
verses.text: When they were a good way from the house of Micah, the men who were in the houses near to Micah’s house were gathered together, and overtook the children of Dan.

verses.name: Judges 18:23
verses.text: They cried to the children of Dan. They turned their faces, and said to Micah, “What ails you, that you come with such a company?”

verses.name: Judges 18:24
verses.text: He said, “You have taken away my gods which I made, and the priest, and have gone away, and what more do I have? How then do you say to me, ‘What ails you?’”

verses.name: Judges 18:25
verses.text: The children of Dan said to him, “Don’t let your voice be heard among us, lest angry fellows fall on you, and you lose your life, with the lives of your household.”

verses.name: Judges 18:26
verses.text: The children of Dan went their way: and when Micah saw that they were too strong for him, he turned and went back to his house.

verses.name: Judges 18:27
verses.text: They took that which Micah had made, and the priest whom he had, and came to Laish, to a people quiet and secure, and struck them with the edge of the sword; and they burnt the city with fire.

verses.name: Judges 18:28
verses.text: There was no deliverer, because it was far from Sidon, and they had no dealings with any man; and it was in the valley that lies by Beth Rehob. They built the city, and lived therein.

verses.name: Judges 18:29
verses.text: They called the name of the city Dan, after the name of Dan their father, who was born to Israel: however the name of the city was Laish at the first.

verses.name: Judges 18:30
verses.text: The children of Dan set up for themselves the engraved image: and Jonathan, the son of Gershom, the son of Moses, he and his sons were priests to the tribe of the Danites until the day of the captivity of the land.

verses.name: Judges 18:31
verses.text: So they set them up Micah’s engraved image which he made, all the time that God’s house was in Shiloh.

verses.name: Judges 19:1
verses.text: In those days, when there was no king in Israel, there was a certain Levite living on the farther side of the hill country of Ephraim, who took to him a concubine out of Bethlehem Judah.

verses.name: Judges 19:2
verses.text: His concubine played the prostitute against him, and went away from him to her father’s house to Bethlehem Judah, and was there the space of four months.

verses.name: Judges 19:3
verses.text: Her husband arose, and went after her, to speak kindly to her, to bring her again, having his servant with him, and a couple of donkeys: and she brought him into her father’s house; and when the father of the young lady saw him, he rejoiced to meet him.

verses.name: Judges 19:4
verses.text: His father-in-law, the young lady’s father, retained him; and he stayed with him three days: so they ate and drink, and lodged there.

verses.name: Judges 19:5
verses.text: On the fourth day, they arose early in the morning, and he rose up to depart. The young lady’s father said to his son-in-law, “Strengthen your heart with a morsel of bread, and afterward you shall go your way.”

verses.name: Judges 19:6
verses.text: So they sat down, ate, and drank, both of them together: and the young lady’s father said to the man, “Please be pleased to stay all night, and let your heart be merry.”

verses.name: Judges 19:7
verses.text: The man rose up to depart; but his father-in-law urged him, and he lodged there again.

verses.name: Judges 19:8
verses.text: He arose early in the morning on the fifth day to depart; and the young lady’s father said, “Please strengthen your heart and stay until the day declines”; and they both ate.

verses.name: Judges 19:9
verses.text: When the man rose up to depart, he, and his concubine, and his servant, his father-in-law, the young lady’s father, said to him, “Behold, now the day draws toward evening, please stay all night: behold, the day grows to an end, lodge here, that your heart may be merry; and tomorrow go on your way early, that you may go home.”

verses.name: Judges 19:10
verses.text: But the man wouldn’t stay that night, but he rose up and departed, and came over against Jebus (the same is Jerusalem): and there were with him a couple of donkeys saddled; his concubine also was with him.

verses.name: Judges 19:11
verses.text: When they were by Jebus, the day was far spent; and the servant said to his master, “Please come and let us turn aside into this city of the Jebusites, and lodge in it.”

verses.name: Judges 19:12
verses.text: His master said to him, “We won’t turn aside into the city of a foreigner, that is not of the children of Israel; but we will pass over to Gibeah.”

verses.name: Judges 19:13
verses.text: He said to his servant, “Come and let us draw near to one of these places; and we will lodge in Gibeah, or in Ramah.”

verses.name: Judges 19:14
verses.text: So they passed on and went their way; and the sun went down on them near to Gibeah, which belongs to Benjamin.

verses.name: Judges 19:15
verses.text: They turned aside there, to go in to lodge in Gibeah: and he went in, and sat him down in the street of the city; for there was no man who took them into his house to lodge.

verses.name: Judges 19:16
verses.text: Behold, there came an old man from his work out of the field at evening: now the man was of the hill country of Ephraim, and he lived in Gibeah; but the men of the place were Benjamites.

verses.name: Judges 19:17
verses.text: He lifted up his eyes, and saw the wayfaring man in the street of the city; and the old man said, “Where are you going? Where did you come from?”

verses.name: Judges 19:18
verses.text: He said to him, “We are passing from Bethlehem Judah to the farther side of the hill country of Ephraim. I am from there, and I went to Bethlehem Judah. I am going to Yahweh’s house; and there is no man who takes me into his house.

verses.name: Judges 19:19
verses.text: Yet there is both straw and provender for our donkeys; and there is bread and wine also for me, and for your handmaid, and for the young man who is with your servants: there is no want of anything.”

verses.name: Judges 19:20
verses.text: The old man said, “Peace be to you; howsoever let all your wants lie on me; only don’t lodge in the street.”

verses.name: Judges 19:21
verses.text: So he brought him into his house, and gave the donkeys fodder; and they washed their feet, and ate and drink.

verses.name: Judges 19:22
verses.text: As they were making their hearts merry, behold, the men of the city, certain base fellows, surrounded the house, beating at the door; and they spoke to the master of the house, the old man, saying, “Bring out the man who came into your house, that we may have sex with him!”

verses.name: Judges 19:23
verses.text: The man, the master of the house, went out to them, and said to them, “No, my brothers, please don’t act so wickedly; since this man has come into my house, don’t do this folly.

verses.name: Judges 19:24
verses.text: Behold, here is my virgin daughter and his concubine. I will bring them out now. Humble them, and do with them what seems good to you; but to this man don’t do any such folly.”

verses.name: Judges 19:25
verses.text: But the men wouldn’t listen to him: so the man laid hold of his concubine, and brought her out to them; and they had sex with her, and abused her all night until the morning: and when the day began to dawn, they let her go.

verses.name: Judges 19:26
verses.text: Then came the woman in the dawning of the day, and fell down at the door of the man’s house where her lord was, until it was light.

verses.name: Judges 19:27
verses.text: Her lord rose up in the morning, and opened the doors of the house, and went out to go his way; and behold, the woman his concubine was fallen down at the door of the house, with her hands on the threshold.

verses.name: Judges 19:28
verses.text: He said to her, “Get up, and let us be going!” but no one answered. Then he took her up on the donkey; and the man rose up, and went to his place.

verses.name: Judges 19:29
verses.text: When he had come into his house, he took a knife, and laid hold on his concubine, and divided her, limb by limb, into twelve pieces, and sent her throughout all the borders of Israel.

verses.name: Judges 19:30
verses.text: It was so, that all who saw it said, “There was no such deed done nor seen from the day that the children of Israel came up out of the land of Egypt to this day! Consider it, take counsel, and speak.”

verses.name: Judges 20:1
verses.text: Then all the children of Israel went out, and the congregation was assembled as one man, from Dan even to Beersheba, with the land of Gilead, to Yahweh at Mizpah.

verses.name: Judges 20:2
verses.text: The chiefs of all the people, even of all the tribes of Israel, presented themselves in the assembly of the people of God, four hundred thousand footmen who drew sword.

verses.name: Judges 20:3
verses.text: (Now the children of Benjamin heard that the children of Israel had gone up to Mizpah.) The children of Israel said, “Tell us, how did this wickedness happen?”

verses.name: Judges 20:4
verses.text: The Levite, the husband of the woman who was murdered, answered, “I came into Gibeah that belongs to Benjamin, I and my concubine, to lodge.

verses.name: Judges 20:5
verses.text: The men of Gibeah rose against me, and surrounded the house by night. They thought to have slain me, and they forced my concubine, and she is dead.

verses.name: Judges 20:6
verses.text: I took my concubine, and cut her in pieces, and sent her throughout all the country of the inheritance of Israel; for they have committed lewdness and folly in Israel.

verses.name: Judges 20:7
verses.text: Behold, you children of Israel, all of you, give here your advice and counsel.”

verses.name: Judges 20:8
verses.text: All the people arose as one man, saying, “None of us will go to his tent, neither will any of us turn to his house.

verses.name: Judges 20:9
verses.text: But now this is the thing which we will do to Gibeah: we will go up against it by lot;

verses.name: Judges 20:10
verses.text: and we will take ten men of one hundred throughout all the tribes of Israel, and one hundred of one thousand, and a thousand out of ten thousand, to get food for the people, that they may do, when they come to Gibeah of Benjamin, according to all the folly that they have worked in Israel.”

verses.name: Judges 20:11
verses.text: So all the men of Israel were gathered against the city, knit together as one man.

verses.name: Judges 20:12
verses.text: The tribes of Israel sent men through all the tribe of Benjamin, saying, “What wickedness is this that is happen among you?

verses.name: Judges 20:13
verses.text: Now therefore deliver up the men, the base fellows, who are in Gibeah, that we may put them to death, and put away evil from Israel.”   But Benjamin would not listen to the voice of their brothers the children of Israel.

verses.name: Judges 20:14
verses.text: The children of Benjamin gathered themselves together out of the cities to Gibeah, to go out to battle against the children of Israel.

verses.name: Judges 20:15
verses.text: The children of Benjamin were numbered on that day out of the cities twenty-six thousand men who drew the sword, besides the inhabitants of Gibeah, who were numbered seven hundred chosen men.

verses.name: Judges 20:16
verses.text: Among all this people there were seven hundred chosen men left-handed; everyone could sling stones at a hair-breadth, and not miss.

verses.name: Judges 20:17
verses.text: The men of Israel, besides Benjamin, were numbered four hundred thousand men who drew sword: all these were men of war.

verses.name: Judges 20:18
verses.text: The children of Israel arose, and went up to Bethel, and asked counsel of God; and they said, “Who shall go up for us first to battle against the children of Benjamin?”   Yahweh said, “Judah first.”

verses.name: Judges 20:19
verses.text: The children of Israel rose up in the morning, and encamped against Gibeah.

verses.name: Judges 20:20
verses.text: The men of Israel went out to battle against Benjamin; and the men of Israel set the battle in array against them at Gibeah.

verses.name: Judges 20:21
verses.text: The children of Benjamin came out of Gibeah, and destroyed down to the ground of the Israelites on that day twenty-two thousand men.

verses.name: Judges 20:22
verses.text: The people, the men of Israel, encouraged themselves, and set the battle again in array in the place where they set themselves in array the first day.

verses.name: Judges 20:23
verses.text: The children of Israel went up and wept before Yahweh until evening; and they asked of Yahweh, saying, “Shall I again draw near to battle against the children of Benjamin my brother?”   Yahweh said, “Go up against him.”

verses.name: Judges 20:24
verses.text: The children of Israel came near against the children of Benjamin the second day.

verses.name: Judges 20:25
verses.text: Benjamin went out against them out of Gibeah the second day, and destroyed down to the ground of the children of Israel again eighteen thousand men; all these drew the sword.

verses.name: Judges 20:26
verses.text: Then all the children of Israel, and all the people, went up, and came to Bethel, and wept, and sat there before Yahweh, and fasted that day until evening; and they offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before Yahweh.

verses.name: Judges 20:27
verses.text: The children of Israel asked of Yahweh (for the ark of the covenant of God was there in those days,

verses.name: Judges 20:28
verses.text: and Phinehas, the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron, stood before it in those days), saying, “Shall I yet again go out to battle against the children of Benjamin my brother, or shall I cease?”   Yahweh said, “Go up; for tomorrow I will deliver him into your hand.”

verses.name: Judges 20:29
verses.text: Israel set ambushes all around Gibeah.

verses.name: Judges 20:30
verses.text: The children of Israel went up against the children of Benjamin on the third day, and set themselves in array against Gibeah, as at other times.

verses.name: Judges 20:31
verses.text: The children of Benjamin went out against the people, and were drawn away from the city; and they began to strike and kill of the people, as at other times, in the highways, of which one goes up to Bethel, and the other to Gibeah, in the field, about thirty men of Israel.

verses.name: Judges 20:32
verses.text: The children of Benjamin said, “They are struck down before us, as at the first.” But the children of Israel said, “Let us flee, and draw them away from the city to the highways.”

verses.name: Judges 20:33
verses.text: All the men of Israel rose up out of their place, and set themselves in array at Baal Tamar: and the ambushers of Israel broke out of their place, even out of Maareh Geba.

verses.name: Judges 20:34
verses.text: There came over against Gibeah ten thousand chosen men out of all Israel, and the battle was severe; but they didn’t know that evil was close on them.

verses.name: Judges 20:35
verses.text: Yahweh struck Benjamin before Israel; and the children of Israel destroyed of Benjamin that day twenty-five thousand one hundred men: all these drew the sword.

verses.name: Judges 20:36
verses.text: So the children of Benjamin saw that they were struck; for the men of Israel gave place to Benjamin, because they trusted the ambushers whom they had set against Gibeah.

verses.name: Judges 20:37
verses.text: The ambushers hurried, and rushed on Gibeah; and the ambushers drew themselves along, and struck all the city with the edge of the sword.

verses.name: Judges 20:38
verses.text: Now the appointed sign between the men of Israel and the ambushers was that they should make a great cloud of smoke rise up out of the city.

verses.name: Judges 20:39
verses.text: The men of Israel turned in the battle, and Benjamin began to strike and kill of the men of Israel about thirty persons; for they said, “Surely they are struck down before us, as in the first battle.”

verses.name: Judges 20:40
verses.text: But when the cloud began to arise up out of the city in a pillar of smoke, the Benjamites looked behind them; and behold, the whole of the city went up in smoke to the sky.

verses.name: Judges 20:41
verses.text: The men of Israel turned, and the men of Benjamin were dismayed; for they saw that evil had come on them.

verses.name: Judges 20:42
verses.text: Therefore they turned their backs before the men of Israel to the way of the wilderness; but the battle followed hard after them; and those who came out of the cities destroyed them in its midst.

verses.name: Judges 20:43
verses.text: They surrounded the Benjamites, chased them, and trod them down at their resting place, as far as over against Gibeah toward the sunrise.

verses.name: Judges 20:44
verses.text: There fell of Benjamin eighteen thousand men; all these were men of valor.

verses.name: Judges 20:45
verses.text: They turned and fled toward the wilderness to the rock of Rimmon: and they gleaned of them in the highways five thousand men, and followed hard after them to Gidom, and struck of them two thousand men.

verses.name: Judges 20:46
verses.text: So that all who fell that day of Benjamin were twenty-five thousand men who drew the sword; all these were men of valor.

verses.name: Judges 20:47
verses.text: But six hundred men turned and fled toward the wilderness to the rock of Rimmon, and stayed in the rock of Rimmon four months.

verses.name: Judges 20:48
verses.text: The men of Israel turned again on the children of Benjamin, and struck them with the edge of the sword, both the entire city, and the livestock, and all that they found: moreover all the cities which they found they set on fire.

verses.name: Judges 21:1
verses.text: Now the men of Israel had sworn in Mizpah, saying, “There shall not any of us give his daughter to Benjamin as wife.”

verses.name: Judges 21:2
verses.text: The people came to Bethel, and sat there until evening before God, and lifted up their voices, and wept severely.

verses.name: Judges 21:3
verses.text: They said, “Yahweh, the God of Israel, why has this happened in Israel, that there should be today one tribe lacking in Israel?”

verses.name: Judges 21:4
verses.text: On the next day, the people rose early, and built there an altar, and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings.

verses.name: Judges 21:5
verses.text: The children of Israel said, “Who is there among all the tribes of Israel who didn’t come up in the assembly to Yahweh?” For they had made a great oath concerning him who didn’t come up to Yahweh to Mizpah, saying, “He shall surely be put to death.”

verses.name: Judges 21:6
verses.text: The children of Israel grieved for Benjamin their brother, and said, “There is one tribe cut off from Israel this day.

verses.name: Judges 21:7
verses.text: How shall we provide wives for those who remain, since we have sworn by Yahweh that we will not give them of our daughters to wives?”

verses.name: Judges 21:8
verses.text: They said, “What one is there of the tribes of Israel who didn’t come up to Yahweh to Mizpah?” Behold, no one came from Jabesh Gilead to the camp to the assembly.

verses.name: Judges 21:9
verses.text: For when the people were numbered, behold, there were none of the inhabitants of Jabesh Gilead there.

verses.name: Judges 21:10
verses.text: The congregation sent there twelve thousand men of the most valiant, and commanded them, saying, “Go and strike the inhabitants of Jabesh Gilead with the edge of the sword, with the women and the little ones.

verses.name: Judges 21:11
verses.text: This is the thing that you shall do: you shall utterly destroy every male, and every woman who has lain with a man.”

verses.name: Judges 21:12
verses.text: They found among the inhabitants of Jabesh Gilead four hundred young virgins, who had not known man by lying with him; and they brought them to the camp to Shiloh, which is in the land of Canaan.

verses.name: Judges 21:13
verses.text: The whole congregation sent and spoke to the children of Benjamin who were in the rock of Rimmon, and proclaimed peace to them.

verses.name: Judges 21:14
verses.text: Benjamin returned at that time; and they gave them the women whom they had saved alive of the women of Jabesh Gilead: and yet so they weren’t enough for them.

verses.name: Judges 21:15
verses.text: The people grieved for Benjamin, because that Yahweh had made a breach in the tribes of Israel.

verses.name: Judges 21:16
verses.text: Then the elders of the congregation said, “How shall we provide wives for those who remain, since the women are destroyed out of Benjamin?”

verses.name: Judges 21:17
verses.text: They said, “There must be an inheritance for those who are escaped of Benjamin, that a tribe not be blotted out from Israel.

verses.name: Judges 21:18
verses.text: However we may not give them wives of our daughters, for the children of Israel had sworn, saying, ‘Cursed is he who gives a wife to Benjamin.’”

verses.name: Judges 21:19
verses.text: They said, “Behold, there is a feast of Yahweh from year to year in Shiloh, which is on the north of Bethel, on the east side of the highway that goes up from Bethel to Shechem, and on the south of Lebonah.”

verses.name: Judges 21:20
verses.text: They commanded the children of Benjamin, saying, “Go and lie in wait in the vineyards,

verses.name: Judges 21:21
verses.text: and see, and behold, if the daughters of Shiloh come out to dance in the dances, then come out of the vineyards, and each man catch his wife of the daughters of Shiloh, and go to the land of Benjamin.

verses.name: Judges 21:22
verses.text: It shall be, when their fathers or their brothers come to complain to us, that we will say to them, ‘Grant them graciously to us, because we didn’t take for each man his wife in battle, neither did you give them to them, otherwise you would now be guilty.’”

verses.name: Judges 21:23
verses.text: The children of Benjamin did so, and took them wives, according to their number, of those who danced, whom they carried off. They went and returned to their inheritance, built the cities, and lived in them.

verses.name: Judges 21:24
verses.text: The children of Israel departed there at that time, every man to his tribe and to his family, and they went out from there every man to his inheritance.

verses.name: Judges 21:25
verses.text: In those days there was no king in Israel: every man did that which was right in his own eyes.
